title: hasAdminTagInLeadingComments: tag inside function body passes check; no test for this case
body_excerpt: The `hasAdminTagInLeadingComments` function (L59–69 of `inngest-admin-tag.mjs`) calls `getAllComments()`, which returns every comment in the file, includi
evidence: eslint-rules/inngest-admin-tag.mjs lines 66-81: implementation now uses `sourceCode.getCommentsBefore(firstNode)` (not `getAllComments()`), anchoring only to file-header comments before the first AST node. eslint-rules/inngest-admin-tag.test.mjs lines 159-167 adds a regression test: 'Regression: a tag buried inside a function body must NOT count as a file-level declaration' — this exact case is covered.

title: router-push-ancestor-chain: push inside callback (forEach/onPress) satisfies check for outer function
body_excerpt: The JSDoc names the conditional-branch limitation but the same walk also descends into FunctionExpression / ArrowFunctionExpression nodes. A router.push(
evidence: apps/mobile/eslint-rules/router-push-ancestor-chain.mjs lines 186-211: `collectPushPathnamesUpTo` walks all child nodes recursively including ArrowFunctionExpression bodies. A `router.push(parent)` inside a `.forEach` callback at range < beforeRange would be collected and satisfy the ancestor-chain check even though it doesn't run before the outer call. No guard added to stop at function boundaries. The JSDoc comment at line 180 acknowledges the conditional-branch limitation but not the callback/nested-function false-negative.
suggested_notion_title: GC4 rule: router.push inside nested callback satisfies ancestor-chain check for outer function
suggested_notion_body:
File: apps/mobile/eslint-rules/router-push-ancestor-chain.mjs
Function: collectPushPathnamesUpTo (lines 186-211)

The walk descends into all child nodes including ArrowFunctionExpression and FunctionExpression bodies. A router.push(parentRoute) inside a .forEach handler or onPress prop would satisfy the ancestor-chain check for the containing function even though that push only runs conditionally/asynchronously.

Fix: Stop walk descent at FunctionExpression / ArrowFunctionExpression boundaries in collectPushPathnamesUpTo (same guard as needed for isInsideTryBlock in safe-non-core.guard.test.ts).

Priority: Medium — 0 violations today, but will silently pass future false-negative cases.

title: GC5 inngest-admin-tag: db.execute(sql) bypasses raw-db detection; one real site exists
body_excerpt: Drizzle exposes db.execute(sql) for raw SQL. This is the highest-risk form of DB access (no schema columns, no ORM query builder) but would silently byp
evidence: eslint-rules/inngest-admin-tag.mjs lines 35-42: RAW_DB_METHODS set contains select/insert/update/delete/query/transaction but NOT execute. apps/api/src/inngest/functions/memory-facts-embed-backfill.ts line 131 uses db.execute(sql...) and that file has no @inngest-admin tag (confirmed by grep). This is both a rule gap (execute not in the set) and a real live violation (the backfill file).
suggested_notion_title: GC5 inngest-admin-tag misses db.execute — real untagged site in memory-facts-embed-backfill.ts
suggested_notion_body:
File: eslint-rules/inngest-admin-tag.mjs + apps/api/src/inngest/functions/memory-facts-embed-backfill.ts

The GC5 rule's RAW_DB_METHODS set omits 'execute'. Drizzle's db.execute() with raw SQL is the highest-risk DB access form (no schema columns, no ORM builder) but is invisible to the ratchet.

Real site: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/memory-facts-embed-backfill.ts:131 calls db.execute() with no @inngest-admin tag and no createScopedRepository import.

Fix: Add 'execute' to RAW_DB_METHODS; add a test case; add @inngest-admin tag to memory-facts-embed-backfill.ts.

title: safe-non-core.guard.test.ts: isInsideTryBlock comment contradicts implementation (empty if-block, walks past function boundaries)
body_excerpt: The comment claims "only the nearest enclosing try counts" but the code walks past all function boundaries. This creates a latent false-negative: an `inn
evidence: apps/api/src/services/safe-non-core.guard.test.ts lines 99-119: the if-block at lines 104-116 that handles function-boundary node types (FunctionDeclaration, FunctionExpression, ArrowFunction, MethodDeclaration) has NO `return false` inside it — it falls through to `cur = cur.parent` unconditionally. The comment at line 103 says 'Stop at function boundaries' but the code does not. An inngest.send() inside an unawaited async callback nested in a try block would pass as 'try-catch' incorrectly.
suggested_notion_title: safe-non-core ratchet: isInsideTryBlock walks past function boundaries — empty if-block bug
suggested_notion_body:
File: apps/api/src/services/safe-non-core.guard.test.ts lines 99-119

The isInsideTryBlock function has an if-block for function boundary nodes (FunctionDeclaration, FunctionExpression, ArrowFunction, MethodDeclaration) but the block body is empty — execution falls through to `cur = cur.parent` unconditionally. The comment says 'Stop at function boundaries — a try in an outer function doesn't count' but this is not enforced.

Fix: Add `return false;` inside the function-boundary if-block, then add a self-check test: an inngest.send() inside a nested arrow function inside a try-catch should classify as 'bare', not 'try-catch'.

title: session-exchange.ts orphan-persist catch: captureException(persistErr) missing alongside safeSend
body_excerpt:
Files: apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts — processMessage catch (~line 1863) and both streamMessage catch blocks (~lines 2043, 2092)

Th
evidence: apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts: captureException(persistErr, { profileId, extra: { phase: 'orphan_persist_failed' } }) is present at lines 2057-2060, 2262-2265, and 2315-2318 — all three orphan-persist catch sites call both safeSend() AND captureException(persistErr). The Sentry observability regression described in the finding has been fixed.

title: safe-non-core.guard.test.ts lines 99-119: empty if-block means walk continues past function boundaries
body_excerpt: See inline comment on `safe-non-core.guard.test.ts` lines 99-119. The opening comment says "Stop at function boundaries" but the if-block is empty - the
evidence: Same as comment-4451530814: apps/api/src/services/safe-non-core.guard.test.ts lines 104-116 — the if-block covering ArrowFunction/FunctionDeclaration ancestor types contains only a comment and no `return false`. This is a duplicate finding on the same code path; still unaddressed.

title: session-exchange.ts orphan-persist: captureException(persistErr) missing at three orphan-persist sites (duplicate finding)
body_excerpt: See inline comment on `session-exchange.ts` lines 1863-1879 (same pattern at the `stream` and `fallback` catch sites). The original code reported `persis
evidence: apps/api/src/services/session/session-exchange.ts: all three orphan-persist catch sites (lines 2057, 2262, 2315) now call captureException(persistErr, { profileId, extra: { phase: 'orphan_persist_failed' } }) alongside safeSend. Duplicate finding; same as comment-4451533651, confirmed addressed.

title: waitForEvent timeout test: captureExceptionSpy restored before assertion (always-fail)
body_excerpt: `captureExceptionSpy.mockRestore()` is called at line 1337. Jest's `mockRestore()` chains through `mockReset()` then `mockClear()`, which zeroes out `mo
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/session-completed.integration.test.ts lines 1378-1428: captureExceptionSpy is created at 1378, assertions at 1421-1426, mockRestore() at 1428. Restore is AFTER assertions — correct order. The finding described the wrong order; current code has it right.

title: Spy leak in memory dedup rollout test: dedupEnabledSpy/rolloutSpy restored inline without try/finally
body_excerpt: `dedupEnabledSpy` and `rolloutSpy` are inline-restored after `handler()`. If `handler()` throws, both spies remain active (`.mockReturnValue(true)`) for
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/session-completed.integration.test.ts lines 1309-1345: dedupEnabledSpy.mockReturnValue(true) at 1311, rolloutSpy.mockReturnValue(true) at 1314. After handler() at 1320-1342, mockRestore() calls at 1344-1345 with no try/finally wrapping the handler() call. If handler() throws (flaky upstream failure), both spies remain active corrupting config flags for subsequent tests. No afterEach(() => jest.restoreAllMocks()) present in the outer describe for this section.
suggested_notion_title: Test spy leak: dedupEnabledSpy/rolloutSpy in session-completed integration test lack try/finally cleanup
suggested_notion_body:
File: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/session-completed.integration.test.ts lines 1309-1345

The 'memory dedup rollout' test spies on config.isMemoryFactsDedupEnabled and config.isProfileInDedupRollout with mockReturnValue(true). Both are restored inline after handler() with no try/finally guard. If handler() throws (network blip, DB error, upstream test pollution), both spies stay active for all subsequent tests in the suite, silently forcing dedup=true and corrupting the rollout check.

Fix: Wrap handler() + assertions in try/finally with mockRestore() in the finally block, or add afterEach(() => jest.restoreAllMocks()) to the outer describe block.

title: session-completed.integration.test.ts: two tests restore spies inline without afterEach/finally
body_excerpt: In `session-completed.integration.test.ts`, two tests restore spies **inline inside the test body** rather than in an `afterEach`/`finally` block
evidence: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/session-completed.integration.test.ts: (1) lines 1309-1351: dedupEnabledSpy/rolloutSpy restored at 1344-1345 without try/finally — confirmed unaddressed. (2) lines 1378-1428: captureExceptionSpy restored at 1428 AFTER assertions (correct order), so the waitForEvent timeout case is actually fixed. The dedup-rollout spy leak remains.

title: verify-test-receipts.sh / record-test-receipt.sh: silent gate bypass when base SHA cannot be resolved
body_excerpt: Both scripts fall back to the literal string main when both merge-base calls fail (verify-test-receipts.sh lines 30-32, record-test-receipt.sh lines 66-6
evidence: Both verify-test-receipts.sh and record-test-receipt.sh are absent from the current working tree (PR #259's receipt infrastructure was superseded by PR #299's blocking pre-push hook in scripts/pre-push-tests.sh). The .test-receipts/ directory and mobile.json are also absent. The gate bypass concern no longer applies as the mechanism was replaced entirely.

title: record-test-receipt.sh: machine hostname written to committed mobile.json — privacy/noise concern
body_excerpt: record-test-receipt.sh writes verifiedBy containing the git username and machine hostname into .test-receipts/mobile.json, which is committed and pushed.
evidence: record-test-receipt.sh and .test-receipts/mobile.json are both absent from the current working tree. The receipt infrastructure from PR #259 was removed when PR #299 replaced it with the blocking pre-push hook. No hostname data is committed.

title: pre-push hook: error message only shows SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK=1 bypass, not --no-verify
body_excerpt: The hook header documents two bypass paths (SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK=1 git push and git push --no-verify), but the error block shown to the developer at runtim
evidence: The receipt-based pre-push hook from PR #259 was replaced by scripts/pre-push-tests.sh (PR #299). The new hook at .husky/pre-push documents only `git push --no-verify` as the skip path; scripts/pre-push-tests.sh lines 17-19 and 30-52 use SKIP_PRE_PUSH=1 (not SKIP_RECEIPT_CHECK). The ambiguous dual-bypass documentation no longer exists.

title: GC6: net-new internal mock added in index.test.tsx while editing (use-progress hook mocked with gc1-allow)
body_excerpt: `index.test.tsx` was edited and a new `jest.mock('../../../../hooks/use-progress', ...)` was added. GC6 requires _reducing_ internal mock sites on every
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/child/[profileId]/index.test.tsx line 105: jest.mock('../../../../hooks/use-progress') with `/* gc1-allow: ... */` annotation is present. The gc1-allow annotation satisfies the lint ratchet but GC6 requires net reduction on each test-file edit, not trading one mock for another. The mock has not been converted to real implementation or jest.requireActual pattern.
suggested_notion_title: GC6 violation: use-progress internal mock added in child/[profileId]/index.test.tsx
suggested_notion_body:
File: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/child/[profileId]/index.test.tsx line 105

jest.mock('../../../../hooks/use-progress') was added with gc1-allow annotation when the file was edited in PR #269. GC6 requires that every test-file edit reduces the internal mock backlog, not adds to it.

Fix: Convert to real implementation using QueryClient + seeded cache, or mock RecentSessionsList at the component boundary instead of the underlying hook. If genuinely unavoidable, record the deferral in the commit message per GC6 rules.

title: test-seed.test.ts: imports split by section banner — imports appear both before and after the banner comment
body_excerpt: The new `fs`, `path`, `ENGAGEMENT_SIGNALS`, and `Database` imports appear before the `// Test Seed Service — Unit Tests` block comment, while `import {
evidence: apps/api/src/services/test-seed.test.ts lines 1-17: fs, path, ENGAGEMENT_SIGNALS, Database imports are at lines 1-5, the banner comment '// Test Seed Service — Unit Tests' is at lines 7-9, then import { seedScenario, ... } from './test-seed' appears at lines 11-17. Imports are split across the banner — not grouped together.
suggested_notion_title: test-seed.test.ts: import block split by section banner comment
suggested_notion_body:
File: apps/api/src/services/test-seed.test.ts lines 1-17

Imports are split across the '// Test Seed Service — Unit Tests' banner: fs/path/ENGAGEMENT_SIGNALS/Database appear before it (lines 1-5), and the seedScenario/resetDatabase imports appear after it (lines 11-17). All imports should appear together either above or below the banner.

Fix: Move the banner above all imports, or group all imports together before the banner.

title: test-seed.test.ts: fs.readFileSync(__dirname, 'test-seed.ts') is fragile — breaks if file moved
body_excerpt:
**3. `fs.readFileSync` in the engagement-signal meta-test is fragile**

`path.join(__dirname, 'test-seed.ts')` breaks silently if either file is moved.
evidence: apps/api/src/services/test-seed.test.ts lines 122-126: fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, 'test-seed.ts'), 'utf8') is present and unchanged. No export of ENGAGEMENT_SIGNALS whitelist from test-seed.ts to replace this filesystem dependency. The test will fail silently if test-seed.ts is renamed or moved.
suggested_notion_title: test-seed.test.ts: fragile fs.readFileSync path — breaks if test-seed.ts is moved
suggested_notion_body:
File: apps/api/src/services/test-seed.test.ts lines 122-126

The engagement-signal meta-test reads test-seed.ts source via fs.readFileSync(path.join(__dirname, 'test-seed.ts')). If test-seed.ts is renamed or moved, this breaks silently (no TypeScript error, just a runtime ENOENT).

Fix: Export the engagement-signal list or a whitelist constant from test-seed.ts and import it directly in the test — same coverage, no filesystem dependency.

title: pre-push-tests.sh L133: nx affected --base=HEAD diffs HEAD against itself — fallback is a no-op
body_excerpt:
File: `scripts/pre-push-tests.sh`
Line: L133
Issue: `nx affected -t test --base=HEAD --exclude=mobile` — when pre-push runs, all commits are already in
evidence: scripts/pre-push-tests.sh lines 140-147: the >100 TS files fallback uses `--base="$AFFECTED_BASE" --head="$AFFECTED_HEAD"` where AFFECTED_BASE is set to either $remote_sha (the previously pushed SHA) or 'origin/main' for new branches (lines 80-88). The literal '--base=HEAD' described in the finding is not present in the current file.

title: .github/pull_request_template.md deleted — Verified-By, Failure modes, Sweep audit sections removed with no replacement
body_excerpt:
File: `.github/pull_request_template.md`
Line: (deleted)
Issue: The template enforced three sections CLAUDE.md explicitly mandates: **Verified-By** (wit
evidence: .github/ directory contains only actions/ and workflows/ subdirectories — pull_request_template.md is absent. git log confirms it was deleted in commit a2e31a606 (PR #299) and never restored. No replacement mechanism (commitlint rule, CI step, or updated template) exists in workflows/.
suggested_notion_title: PR template deleted in #299 — Failure modes + Sweep audit sections have no replacement
suggested_notion_body:
File: .github/pull_request_template.md (deleted in commit a2e31a606, PR #299)

The PR template enforced three CLAUDE.md-mandated sections: Verified-By, Failure modes considered, and Sweep audit. The pre-push hook from PR #299 automates tsc/jest validation making Verified-By checkboxes redundant, but Failure modes and Sweep audit cannot be automated — they require human documentation.

No replacement exists: .github/workflows/ has no commitlint rule or CI step requiring these sections.

Fix: Restore a lightweight template retaining ## Failure modes considered, ## Sweep audit, and ## Notes for reviewers. Remove the now-automated Verified-By checkbox rows since the hook handles them.

title: pull_request_template.md silently deleted — no mention in PR description, Verified-By + failure modes + sweep audit lost
body_excerpt:
File: `.github/pull_request_template.md`
Line: (deleted)
Issue: File is silently deleted with no mention in the PR description. The template enforced t
evidence: Same as comment-4466371099: .github/pull_request_template.md is absent from the working tree. Duplicate finding on the same deletion. No rationale documented anywhere in the repo for the removal.

title: pull_request_template.md: Failure modes considered, Sweep audit, Notes for reviewers sections removed with no automated replacement
body_excerpt:
File: `.github/pull_request_template.md`
Line: N/A (file deleted)
Issue: The template contained three sections not replaced by the blocking hook: `## F
evidence: Same deletion confirmed: .github/pull_request_template.md absent, no workflow or hook replaces the non-automatable sections. Triplicate finding; same root cause as comment-4466371099 and comment-4466386540.
